commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The PR needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for blocking code review findings and real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not request repair automation while contributor real setup proof is missing.; Needs attention: The patch bypasses the existing Dreams symlink/non-file guard by writing directly to DREAMS.md.; Review finding: [P1] Use the Dreams writer for deep summaries — extensions/memory-core/src/dr... (sha=bb68daae7ad78cfd36e96f4cd962c496800ed07d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes memory-core deep dreaming output to always write or replace a `## Deep Sleep` section in `DREAMS.md` and adds focused markdown tests for report writing and section replacement.

PR surface: Source +50, Tests +69. Total +119 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main documents a Deep Sleep summary in the Dreams file, but `writeDeepDreamingReport` only writes the separate report path. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for blocking code review findings and real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not request repair automation while contributor real setup proof is missing.

target.title: fix(memory-core): write ## Deep Sleep summary to DREAMS.md
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The Deep Sleep phase writes per-phase report files (`memory/dreaming/deep/YYYY-MM-DD.md`) but never writes the documented `## Deep Sleep` summary section to `DREAMS.md`. Per docs: "Deep phase writes a `## Deep Sleep` summary into `DREAMS.md` and optionally writes `memory/dreaming/deep/YYYY-MM-DD.md`."
- What changed: `writeDeepDreamingReport` now always writes the `## Deep Sleep` summary to `DREAMS.md`. When an existing `## Deep Sleep` section is present, it is replaced in-place (including when the heading appears at file position 0 without a leading newline). Other sections in DREAMS.md are preserved. The separate per-phase report file is still conditionally written when storage mode requests it. Added test coverage for separateReports=false (DREAMS.md only) and section replacement (non-destructive, no duplicates).

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Linked Issues

- Closes #91051

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Deep Sleep phase never writes `## Deep Sleep` to DREAMS.md (#91051).

**Real environment tested:** Node v22.22.0, Linux x86_64.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=4 pnpm test -- extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-markdown.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts

**Evidence after fix (terminal capture):**

Test suite (console output — 50 tests pass, including new DREAMS.md assertions and section-replacement test):
```
$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=4 pnpm test -- extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-markdown.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  50 passed (50)
   Duration  7.07s
exit code: 0
```

**Observed result after fix:** `writeDeepDreamingReport` now always writes the `## Deep Sleep` section to DREAMS.md with the body lines. When separateReports is enabled, the per-phase file is also written. When an existing `## Deep Sleep` section is present (including at file start), it is replaced in-place — other sections preserved, no duplicate headings. When separateReports is disabled, DREAMS.md is still written and `undefined` is returned.

**What was not tested:** Live cron-triggered deep dreaming sweep.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
target.author: chengzhichao-xydt
